name: Omakase By Aung
tagline: Burmese-Japanese chef, 8 seats, no menu — just trust and exceptional fish.
mustOrder: omakase — there is no menu, the chef decides based on what's fresh
parking: Street parking on Kapahulu Ave; side streets off Winam Ave
talkStory: Chef Aung Thein Hlaing, born in Myanmar and trained in Tokyo, opened this 8-seat Kapahulu counter around 2021. His edomae-style sushi incorporates Burmese spice knowledge — subtle heat and fermented flavors that differentiate his nigiri from traditional Japanese preparation. Fish sourced daily from Honolulu Fish Auction. The intimate format means direct chef-diner conversation throughout the 15-20 course meal ($150-250). No signage outside — you have to know the address. Book via Instagram DM weeks ahead.
